title: scutellaria multicaulis subsp. koelzii
intro: Scutellaria multicaulis subsp. koelzii, commonly known as Baikal skullcap, is a plant species of the genus Scutellaria, which is a type of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of China and Russia, particularly in the Lake Baikal area. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Baikal skullcap include reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and potentially therapeutic effects on various diseases, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule.
